compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
G3 container coverage review (parent 3fe1 + this core-leaves sweep child): clean — no findings.

This child uniquely covers the `__init__.py` / `reducer/` / `graph/` / `_store/` slice of parent AC3 (the ~100 unguarded broad-except sites remediated to the observability floor in batches; no silent `except ...: pass` without justification), and contributes an AC4 instance (the `__init__.py:228` `_json_or` narrow-to `json.JSONDecodeError` WITH a regression test — the opportunistic-narrowing-with-test pattern), plus an SC2 contribution (the `_store/push.py`, `_store/sync.py`, `reducer/_processors.py` stderr-print→named-logger migration, with stdout data prints untouched).

4-bucket map vs parent criteria for THIS pairing:
- AC1 (substrate + stdout-purity test): structural — covered by sibling 8fbd.
- AC2 (exemplar + enable BLE001/T201 + shrinking allowlist): structural — covered by 16a1.
- AC3 (~100 sites in batches): partial (core-leaves slice here); remainder covered by sweeps d360 (grounding/_engine_support), ceaa (mcp_server/llm), fd72 (_cli/_commands), 0569 (reconciler).
- AC4 (narrowing surfaced a bug → fixed with regression test): partial (the `_json_or` narrow-with-test); also ceaa, 0569, and pattern set by 16a1.
- AC5 (allowlist emptied; lint+tests pass; children parented w/ file-impact): partial — this child removes its own packages from the allowlist; final emptying + gate by 0ec1; all children are parented to 3fe1 with file_impact set.
- SC1 (one lint-enforced way): contributory — established by 16a1, finalized by 0ec1.
- SC2 (silent failures observable; KI/SE never swallowed; stdout pure): partial here; also 8fbd + sibling sweeps (the Base> KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit landmines live in d360/0569).
- SC3 (future audit clean; new violations fail CI): structural — gate enabled by 16a1, final audit by 0ec1.

Absence cross-check against the whole sibling roster: no parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered, and no coverage gap is attributable to this pairing. The AC3 package partition (this child + d360 + ceaa + fd72 + 0569) tiles the codebase with no obviously-uncovered package.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: G3 container coverage (parent 3fe1 + this core-leaves sweep child): CLEAN — no findings. This child covers the __init__.py/reducer/graph/_store slice of AC3, contributes the AC4 _json_or narrow-with-test instance, and the SC2 _store/reducer stderr-print→logger migration. All other parent criteria are covered structurally by named siblings (8fbd substrate/AC1, 16a1 exemplar/AC2, d360+ceaa+fd72+0569 remaining AC3 sweeps, 0ec1 allowlist-empty/final-gate AC5/SC3). Absence cross-check vs whole roster finds no uncovered parent criterion attributable to this pairing.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
G4 child-consistency review (parent 3fe1 + this core-leaves sweep child; whole sibling roster considered). This child migrates stderr-diagnostic prints in `reducer/`/`_store/` to the named logger and brings broad-excepts in `__init__.py`/`reducer/`/`graph/`/`_store/` to the observability floor. Three high-confidence cross-child findings (consumer impact; conflicting assumptions; consumer impact). Other modes clean (see end).

---
FINDING 1 — Consumer impact: a positive stderr-assertion test directly consumes the very `reducer/_processors.py` corrupt-event diagnostic this child migrates to the named logger; migration will break it unless the test is also updated, contradicting this child's "full test suite pass" AC.

criteria: [this child AC3 "Stderr prints in `_store/` and `reducer/` migrate to `logging.getLogger(__name__)`"; this child What "Migrate stderr-diagnostic prints in ... `reducer/_processors.py` to the named logger"; this child AC4 "make lint + full test suite pass"; parent SC2 "failures that used to vanish silently are now observable on a stderr logger"]
location: src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py:578 `print(f"WARNING: skipping corrupt event {filepath}", file=sys.stderr)` is consumed by tests/scripts/reducer/test_reducer_core.py:656 `test_reducer_emits_warning_for_corrupt_event` (asserts `"WARNING" in captured.err` and `"corrupt" in captured.err.lower()` via the `capsys` fixture, after calling `reducer.reduce_ticket(ticket_dir)` directly).
evidence:
  - The test invokes `reduce_ticket` directly — no CLI/MCP entrypoint runs, so the only handler on the `rebar` root logger is the `NullHandler` installed in `__init__.py` by the substrate (8fbd). A migrated `logging.getLogger(__name__).warning("skipping corrupt event ...")` therefore produces NO stderr output in this test context → `captured.err` is empty → `assert "WARNING" in captured.err` FAILS.
  - `capsys` captures `sys.stderr` at the fd/stream level; the substrate's stderr `StreamHandler` is installed only at the CLI/MCP `main` (8fbd file_impact {__init__.py, cli.py, _cli/__init__.py, mcp_server.py}), not in unit-test direct-call paths — so even the "WARNING" token (which comes from the print string today, not a log formatter) is gone after migration.
scenarios:
  - Child migrates `_processors.py:578` to the logger but does not update test_reducer_core.py:656 → the test fails → this child's AC4 "full test suite pass" is unmet.
  - Child instead keeps the print + adds `# noqa: T201` to satisfy lint → AC3 "Stderr prints in reducer/ migrate" is unmet and parent SC2 observability for this site is not actually improved.
impact: This child's print->logger migration of a site that a positive `capsys` stderr-assertion test directly consumes is a guaranteed test break unless the test is co-migrated to `caplog`. The child's description and ACs do not mention updating this consumer test.
checklist_item: consumer impact
suggested_fix: Add to this child's scope an update of tests/scripts/reducer/test_reducer_core.py::test_reducer_emits_warning_for_corrupt_event to assert via `caplog` (record on the `rebar.reducer._processors` logger at WARNING) instead of `capsys`/stderr text, and add an AC bullet that consumer tests of migrated diagnostics are updated to assert on log records.

---
FINDING 2 — Conflicting assumptions: this child's What under-enumerates the `reducer/` print sites it must migrate (names only `reducer/_processors.py`), but `reducer/_cache.py` and `reducer/marker.py` also hold stderr prints in the same package; since AC4 removes `reducer/` from the T201 allowlist and `reducer/` is library/core (T201 banned), every one of those prints must be migrated or carry a noqa, or enable-time/lint goes red.

criteria: [this child What "Migrate stderr-diagnostic prints in `_store/push.py`, `_store/sync.py`, `reducer/_processors.py` to the named logger"; this child AC3 "Stderr prints in `_store/` and `reducer/` migrate"; this child AC4 "These packages are removed from the allowlist; make lint ... pass"; convention "T201 ... banned in library/core/MCP"]
location: reducer/ stderr prints NOT named in the child's What: src/rebar/reducer/_cache.py:42 (`print(... "failed to write cache for {ticket_dir}", file=sys.stderr)`) and src/rebar/reducer/marker.py:31 + marker.py:58 (`print(... ".archived marker ...", file=sys.stderr)`). grep confirms `reducer/` has print( sites at _cache.py:42, marker.py:31, marker.py:58, _processors.py:144, _processors.py:578; `_store/` print sites at sync.py:77/127/144, push.py:128/141/165/181/190/195.
evidence:
  - grep `noqa: T201` in src/rebar/reducer and src/rebar/_store returns ZERO matches — so NONE of these print sites are pre-annotated; once `reducer/`/`_store/` leave the T201 allowlist (AC4), each unmigrated/un-noqa'd print is a fresh T201 violation.
  - The What enumerates only `_processors.py` for reducer; `_cache.py` and `marker.py` (same package, same child file_impact `reducer/`) are silently in-scope per AC3's broader "Stderr prints in `reducer/` migrate" wording but omitted from the actionable What — a within-child inconsistency that risks an author migrating only `_processors.py` and red-CIing the rest on allowlist removal.
  - Note `_processors.py:144` (`PARENT_CHAIN_FORK_RESOLVED`) is ALSO a reducer stderr print not called out for migrate-vs-keep handling (see Finding 3).
impact: The child's enumerated print-migration scope (What) is narrower than the package it makes T201-conformant (AC3/AC4); the unenumerated `_cache.py`/`marker.py` (and `_processors.py:144`) prints will trip T201 the moment `reducer/` is removed from the allowlist, failing this child's own "make lint pass" AC.
checklist_item: conflicting assumptions
suggested_fix: Reconcile the What with AC3 by enumerating ALL reducer/_store stderr-diagnostic print sites in scope (`reducer/_cache.py:42`, `reducer/marker.py:31,58`, `reducer/_processors.py:144,578`, `_store/sync.py:77,127,144`, `_store/push.py:128,141,165,181,190,195`) and stating each is migrated or carries a justified `# noqa: T201` before the package is removed from the allowlist.

---
FINDING 3 — Consumer impact: the `reducer/_processors.py:144` `PARENT_CHAIN_FORK_RESOLVED ...` stderr line is a structured machine-grep-style diagnostic (a stable token, not prose), so migrating it to the logger changes its surface/observability; the child does not classify it as keep-as-diagnostic vs migrate, and any external consumer grepping for that token over stderr would break.

criteria: [this child What "Migrate stderr-diagnostic prints in ... `reducer/_processors.py` to the named logger"; this child AC3 "Stderr prints in `reducer/` migrate"; parent SC2 "failures ... observable on a stderr logger"; convention "stdout is sacred ... only stderr diagnostics migrate"]
location: src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py:144-150 `print(f"PARENT_CHAIN_FORK_RESOLVED ticket={...} winner={...} dropped=[...] loser_env_id=[...]", file=sys.stderr)`.
evidence:
  - The line emits a fixed leading token `PARENT_CHAIN_FORK_RESOLVED` followed by key=value fields — the shape of a diagnostic intended to be greppable/parseable on stderr (the same class of contract that triggered the reconciler `^ERROR:` finding on sibling 0569). grep for `PARENT_CHAIN_FORK_RESOLVED` shows the producer at _processors.py:145 and no in-repo consumer test, but the token shape strongly implies an out-of-band operator/audit grep contract (it is not a casual warning).
  - The child treats all `_processors.py` prints as undifferentiated "stderr-diagnostic prints" to migrate; it does not flag this structured token for keep-or-reformat. After migration the leading token is prefixed by the handler's log formatter (level/logger-name), so a `^PARENT_CHAIN_FORK_RESOLVED` anchored grep breaks even with a handler installed.
impact: A structured stderr diagnostic that reads as a machine token is migrated wholesale without a keep/reformat decision; if any operator/audit tooling greps that token on stderr (its shape implies so), migration silently breaks the consumer — a consumer-impact mode this pairing does not address.
checklist_item: consumer impact
suggested_fix: Explicitly classify `_processors.py:144 PARENT_CHAIN_FORK_RESOLVED` in this child: either (a) keep it as a structured stderr print with a justified `# noqa: T201` (preserving the token contract), or (b) migrate it AND verify no anchored/`^`-prefixed grep consumer depends on the bare token (preserve the token as the log message body, no leading formatter token a consumer anchors on). Add a one-line note recording the keep-vs-migrate decision for structured-token prints.

---
Modes checked and clean for this pairing: implicit shared state (this child removes its own packages from the BLE001/T201 allowlist; the allowlist is sequenced via blocks edges 16a1 -> 8d01 -> 0ec1, so no concurrent writer at this child's time — 16a1 authors it, 0ec1 empties it); scope overlap (the child's packages __init__.py/reducer/graph/_store are touched by no sweep sibling; 8fbd also touches __init__.py but only adds the NullHandler with explicitly "no except changes," distinct from this child's `_json_or` narrow, and correctly ordered 8fbd->16a1->8d01); ordering violation (this child needs the logger substrate (8fbd) and the convention+allowlist (16a1) first; both are encoded as inbound blocks, chain consistent); dependency gap (the un-owned `_io/` package — 7 BLE001 sites — is a real roster hole but is NOT attributable to THIS pairing: it is outside this child's stated `__init__/reducer/graph/_store` scope and is already filed against 16a1 (allowlist author) and 0ec1 (allowlist-empty gate); I do not re-attribute it here per the absence cross-check); residual references (the negative-assertion tests at tests/scripts/test_ticket_unblock.py:840 and tests/scripts/graph/test_graph_cycles.py:318 reference the migrated `skipping corrupt event` string but assert it is ABSENT — they remain green after migration regardless, so benign, not a break). The three findings above are the consumer-impact (x2) and conflicting-assumptions modes.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
G4 child-consistency re-review (parent 3fe1 + this core-leaves sweep child; whole sibling roster considered). Re-verified against the codebase; three high-confidence cross-child findings stand (consumer impact x2; conflicting assumptions). Other modes clean.

FINDING 1 — Consumer impact. The positive stderr-assertion test tests/scripts/reducer/test_reducer_core.py:656 `test_reducer_emits_warning_for_corrupt_event` calls `reducer.reduce_ticket(ticket_dir)` directly and asserts `"WARNING" in captured.err` (capsys) — verified at lines 683-689. The diagnostic it consumes is exactly src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py:578 `print(f"WARNING: skipping corrupt event {filepath}", file=sys.stderr)`, which this child migrates to `logging.getLogger(__name__)`. In a direct-call unit context no entrypoint handler is installed (only the `rebar`-root NullHandler from substrate 8fbd), so a migrated `logger.warning(...)` produces no stderr output → `captured.err` empty → assert fails. This child's AC "make lint + full test suite pass" is then unmet unless the test is co-migrated to `caplog`. Criteria: this child AC3 (reducer prints migrate) / AC4 (full test suite pass) / parent SC2. Suggested fix: add to this child's scope updating test_reducer_emits_warning_for_corrupt_event to assert via `caplog` on logger `rebar.reducer._processors` at WARNING, plus an AC bullet that consumer tests of migrated diagnostics are updated.

FINDING 2 — Conflicting assumptions. The What enumerates only `reducer/_processors.py` for reducer print migration, but grep confirms additional reducer stderr prints at src/rebar/reducer/_cache.py:42, src/rebar/reducer/marker.py:31, src/rebar/reducer/marker.py:58, and src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py:144 — none carry `# noqa: T201`. AC4 removes `reducer/` from the allowlist and `reducer/` is library/core where T201 is banned; so every unmigrated/un-noqa'd print becomes a fresh T201 violation the moment the package leaves the allowlist, failing this child's own "make lint pass" AC. The actionable What is narrower than the package AC3/AC4 makes conformant. Criteria: this child What vs AC3/AC4 + convention (T201 banned in library/core). Suggested fix: enumerate ALL in-scope reducer/_store print sites (reducer/_cache.py:42, reducer/marker.py:31,58, reducer/_processors.py:144,578, _store/sync.py:77,127,144, _store/push.py:128,141,165,181,190,195) and state each is migrated or carries a justified noqa before allowlist removal.

FINDING 3 — Consumer impact. src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py:144-150 emits a structured machine token `PARENT_CHAIN_FORK_RESOLVED ticket=... winner=... dropped=[...] loser_env_id=[...]` (verified) — a greppable/parseable diagnostic, not casual prose. The child treats all `_processors.py` prints as undifferentiated stderr diagnostics to migrate, with no keep-vs-migrate decision. After migration a log formatter prefixes the line (level/logger name), so any anchored/`^PARENT_CHAIN_FORK_RESOLVED` operator/audit grep over stderr breaks. Criteria: this child What/AC3 + parent SC2/stdout-is-sacred. Suggested fix: explicitly classify this site — keep as structured stderr print with justified `# noqa: T201`, OR migrate while preserving the bare token as the log message body and confirming no anchored grep consumer depends on a leading token; record the decision.

Modes clean: implicit shared state (allowlist writes sequenced 16a1→8d01→0ec1 via blocks edges, no concurrent writer); scope overlap (this child's packages untouched by other sweeps; 8fbd touches __init__.py only for NullHandler with no except change, ordered 8fbd→16a1→8d01); ordering violation (substrate 8fbd + convention/allowlist 16a1 are inbound prerequisites, chain consistent); dependency gap (the un-owned `_io/` BLE001 sites are a roster hole but outside this child's stated __init__/reducer/graph/_store scope and already attributable to 16a1/0ec1, not re-attributed here); residual references (negative-assertion tests test_ticket_unblock.py:840 and test_graph_cycles.py:318 reference the `skipping corrupt event` string but assert ABSENCE — verified green after migration, benign).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
**PASS 1 plan-review — criterion G1G2 (Edit-set / scope accuracy)**

**Affirmations (criteria this chunk passes):**
- Every named file/symbol/line VERIFIED to exist: `src/rebar/__init__.py` `_json_or` (actually at line 230, plan says 228 — off by 2 but the symbol exists); the 7 `_io/import_ndjson.py` `# noqa: BLE001` sites at EXACTLY lines 225, 237, 243, 258, 271, 276, 289; stderr prints in `_store/push.py` (128,141,165,181,190,195), `_store/sync.py` (77,127,144), `reducer/_processors.py` (144, 578). No hallucinated targets.
- The allowlist removal target is accurate: `pyproject.toml` `[tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores]` carries `__init__.py`=`[BLE001]`, `reducer/*`=`[T201]`, `graph/*`=`[BLE001,T201]`, `_store/*`=`[BLE001,T201]`, `_io/*`=`[T201]` — all five packages exist, tagged "swept by 8d01".
- Blast-radius (public `import rebar` hot path at `_json_or`) is explicitly acknowledged with a guarded narrow-or-keep-broad strategy — acknowledged high blast-radius is not a fail.

**Findings:**

- [ ] **Enumerate ALL `reducer/` print sites the `reducer/*` T201-allowlist removal will newly flag, not just `_processors.py`.** The "What" names only `reducer/_processors.py` for print migration, but the `reducer/*` entry is `[T201]` and AC5 ("removed from the allowlist; `make lint` passes") + AC4 ("Stderr prints in `_store/` and `reducer/` migrate to `logging.getLogger(__name__)`") cover the WHOLE package. Grep finds in-scope stderr-diagnostic prints the plan does not enumerate: `reducer/_cache.py:42` (cache-write OSError warning), `reducer/marker.py:31` and `:58` (marker write/remove OSError warnings), and a SECOND `_processors.py` print at line 578 (`skipping corrupt event`). With `reducer/*`=`[T201]` removed, every one of these becomes a T201 violation, so `make lint` will fail unless they are also migrated/justified. _location: plan "What" §3 + AC4/AC5; `src/rebar/reducer/_cache.py:42`, `src/rebar/reducer/marker.py:31,58`, `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py:578`._ _criteria: G1G2._ _Suggested fix: broaden the "What" bullet to "migrate/justify ALL stderr prints under `reducer/` (`_cache.py`, `marker.py`, `_processors.py`) so the `reducer/*` T201 ignore can be removed" — or add an explicit enumeration of every print site in scope._

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review (E4 — assumption/premise verification). Verified each codebase assertion by Read/Grep:

- VERIFIED: `_io/import_ndjson.py` carries inline `# noqa: BLE001` at lines 225, 237, 243, 258, 271, 276, 289 (read confirms 225/237/243/258/271/276/289).
- VERIFIED: stderr-diagnostic prints exist in `_store/push.py` (lines 128/141/165/181/190/195), `_store/sync.py` (77/127/144), and `reducer/_processors.py` (144, and 578 = `file=sys.stderr`).
- VERIFIED: `_json_or` swallow exists — function at `__init__.py:230`, `except Exception: return default` at line 235 (plan cites :228, which is the comment header — minor line drift).
- GAP (load-bearing config mismatch): The plan says it will bring broad-excepts in `reducer/` and `_io/` to the floor and "remove these packages from the BLE001/T201 allowlist." But `pyproject.toml` per-file-ignores (lines 208-212) show: `__init__.py` = [BLE001] only; `reducer/*` = [T201] only; `_io/*` = [T201] only; only `graph/*` and `_store/*` carry [BLE001, T201]. So `reducer/*` and `_io/*` are NOT in the BLE001 allowlist — the `_io/import_ndjson.py` BLE001 suppressions are INLINE per-line noqas, not package-level. The AC "these packages are removed from the allowlist" therefore needs to distinguish which rule is removed per package, and the `_io/` BLE re-validation closes inline noqas (not an allowlist entry).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 (E4 — assumption/premise verification) review. Verified plan assertions against the codebase:

VERIFIED (affirmations):
- `_io/import_ndjson.py` 7 `# noqa: BLE001` sites confirmed at lines 225,237,243,258,271,276,289 — exact match, all per-iteration fail-open with `warn(...)`.
- Stderr prints confirmed in `_store/push.py` (lines 129,144,167,184,192,195) and `_store/sync.py` (80,130,147) and `reducer/_processors.py` (144,578, both `file=sys.stderr`).
- `_json_or` broad swallow exists in `__init__.py` (`except Exception: return default`).
- Allowlist confirmed at `pyproject.toml` `[tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores]` (lines 207-212).

FINDINGS (see checklist items below):
1. The `_json_or` swallow is asserted at `__init__.py:228`, but line 228 is a comment; the actual `try/except Exception/return default` is at lines 233-236.
2. Allowlist scope mismatch: `__init__.py` is exempt from `["BLE001"]` ONLY (not T201) and `reducer/*` from `["T201"]` ONLY (not BLE001). The plan's blanket "Remove these packages from the BLE001/T201 allowlist" over-states the per-package rule set.

- [ ] Correct the `_json_or` line citation: the swallow is at `src/rebar/__init__.py:233-236` (`try/except Exception/return default`), not line 228 (a comment). Update the plan body and AC.
- [ ] Reconcile the allowlist-removal claim with the actual per-file rule sets (`pyproject.toml:208-212`): `__init__.py` carries BLE001 only; `reducer/*` carries T201 only; `_io/*` carries T201 only; only `graph/*` and `_store/*` carry both. The plan's "remove from BLE001/T201 allowlist" should name the exact rule(s) per package.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: PASS2 note (idx0): src/rebar/__init__.py:228 is a comment; _json_or defined L230, try/except at L233-240 and ALREADY narrowed to _json.JSONDecodeError (not `except Exception`). Line citation imprecise per finding; but the surrounding-code characterization in the finding's evidence is itself stale.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Core-leaves sweep landed (commits 79164943 + a7ca5a0c). Plan-review PASS (0 blocking, 2 advisory — both citation-accuracy notes: _json_or is at L233-236 not L228, and the per-package allowlist code-sets differ by package; both already matched in the implementation). _json_or narrowed to JSONDecodeError + regression test (epic AC4 — unmasks a previously-hidden TypeError). All graph reduce_ticket fallbacks + _engine-adjacent fallbacks justified-noqa; stderr diagnostics in reducer/_store/_io/graph._links migrated to named loggers; stdout data prints kept (noqa T201). 7 _io/import_ndjson BLE001 noqas re-validated. Core-leaves removed from allowlist. Two tests updated for the library-logging convention (caplog instead of capsys for the corrupt-event warning; dynamic allowlist-path lookup). Full suite 3207 passed/12 skipped; make lint green. Code-review subagent: clean.

compiled_state.description:
# Sweep: core leaves (__init__.py, reducer/, graph/, _store/) to the observability floor

Mostly low-risk fallback/skip patterns (assign-None on corrupt ticket, return-default on parse failure).
ONE careful site: the public-API swallow in `src/rebar/__init__.py:228` (`_json_or` returns a sentinel on
any parse failure) — narrow to `json.JSONDecodeError` only WITH a test, since it is in the `import rebar`
hot path; otherwise keep broad + log.

## What
- Bring broad-excepts in `src/rebar/__init__.py`, `src/rebar/reducer/`, `src/rebar/graph/`,
  `src/rebar/_store/`, and `src/rebar/_io/` to the observability floor (log `exc_info` or justified
  `# noqa: BLE001`).
- **`_io/` coverage (plan-review G4):** `src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py` carries 7 pre-seeded
  `# noqa: BLE001` sites (lines 225,237,243,258,271,276,289 — "one bad row/link never aborts the run").
  These are owned by NO other sweep, so without this they are never re-validated and close-out's
  "empty the allowlist" is unreachable. Re-validate each (they are legitimate per-iteration fail-open —
  keep broad + log at WARN with a justified reason).
- Migrate stderr-diagnostic prints in `_store/push.py`, `_store/sync.py`, `reducer/_processors.py` to the
  named logger.
- Remove these packages from the BLE001/T201 allowlist as they become conformant.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Broad-excepts in `__init__.py`, `reducer/`, `graph/`, `_store/`, `_io/` meet the observability floor.
- [ ] The 7 `_io/import_ndjson.py` `# noqa: BLE001` sites are re-validated (kept broad + logged with a
      justified reason, as legitimate per-iteration fail-open).
- [ ] `__init__.py:228` `_json_or` is narrowed to `json.JSONDecodeError` with a regression test, OR kept
      broad-and-logged with a justification.
- [ ] Stderr prints in `_store/` and `reducer/` migrate to `logging.getLogger(__name__)`.
- [ ] These packages are removed from the allowlist; `make lint` + full test suite pass.

file_impact.reason: _json_or narrowed to json.JSONDecodeError (opportunistic narrowing + regression test)
reduce_ticket fallbacks → justified noqa BLE001; _links.py stderr diagnostics → logger, stdout redirect record kept (noqa T201)
push/sync/hlc stderr diagnostics → logger; best-effort push + HLC fallback broad-but-logged
import_ndjson diagnostics → logger + 7 BLE001 noqas re-validated; _cli.py CLI prints noqa T201
remove core-leaves entries from the shrinking allowlist

compiled_state.title: Sweep: core leaves (__init__, reducer, graph, _store) to observability floor
